content.full_text:
February 28, 1992

Debbie Lozito
Administrative Assistant
Engineers for Furthering Organizational Representation and Training
660 J Street, Suite 445
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-73

Dear Ms. Lozito:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance provided to you under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Except as noted below, your letter dated January 17, 1992, correctly summarizes the information I provided to you. The campaign funds held by the Engineers for Furthering Organizational Representation and Training PAC, which have been used primarily for making contributions to candidates and for administrative expenses, may also be used for the advocacy of issues reasonably related to a political, legislative or governmental purpose of the committee. Enclosed is a recent advice letter which addresses a similar question and may be helpful to you. (Brown Advice Letter, No. I-90-412.) However, as I clarified by telephone on February 12, 1992, the expenditures made by the PAC should be reported on Schedule E of the Form 420 not the Form 620 as indicated in your letter.

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91915. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
